title: Avoid weak hashing algorithms
description: Weak hashing algorithms like MD5, SHA-1, or weak custom hash functions are vulnerable to collision attacks and other cryptographic weaknesses. Using them for security-sensitive operations compromises data integrity and authenticity.
recommendation: Use strong hashing algorithms like SHA-256 or SHA-3 for security-sensitive operations.

noncompliant.description: Using weak hashing algorithms like MD5, SHA-1, or simple checksums for security-sensitive operations makes systems vulnerable to attacks.

compliant.description: Using strong hashing algorithms like SHA-256, SHA-3, or BLAKE2b ensures cryptographic security and resistance to collision attacks.
